Work.definition: exert oneself by doing mental or physical work for a purpose or out of necessity

Colleague.definition: an associate that one works with

Class.definition: a collection of things sharing a common attribute
a body of students who are taught together

Exam.definition: a set of questions or exercises evaluating skill or knowledge

Project.definition: any piece of work that is undertaken or attempted

Classmate.definition: an acquaintance that you go to school with

Teacher.definition: a person whose occupation is teaching

Office.definition: place of business where professional or clerical duties are performed

Lecture.definition: a speech that is open to the public
teaching by giving a discourse on some subject (typically to a class)

Study.definition: applying the mind to learning and understanding a subject (especially by reading)

Boss.definition: a person who exercises control over workers

University.definition: the body of faculty and students at a university

Client.definition: someone who pays for goods or services

Professor.definition: someone who is a member of the faculty at a college or university

Library.definition: a room where books are kept

Grade.definition: assign a grade or rank to, according to one's evaluation

Deadline.definition: the point in time at which something must be completed

Report.definition: a written document describing the findings of some individual or group

Presentation.definition: the activity of formally presenting something (as a prize or reward)
